---
title: State DMV REAL-ID Attribute Service — Adapter Technical Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: state-dmv-realid-attribute-service
adapter-class: conformance
mission-class: identity
status: reserved
phase: phase-planning
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/adapter-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-05-07'
---

# State DMV REAL-ID Attribute Service — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `reserved` · **Class:** `conformance` · **Mission:** `identity` · **Phase:** `phase-planning`

**Canon source:** `canon/adapter-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The State DMV REAL-ID Attribute Service Adapter is the **state-issued driver-license attribute observer** of the UIAO substrate. As a conformance-class identity adapter, it observes attribute-disclosure events from state DMVs — the per-state authorities of record for driver licenses and state IDs under the **REAL ID Act of 2005** — and emits canonical claims with the disclosure-audit envelope.

Federal-state attribute exchange flows through state-operated **AAMVA channels** (the American Association of Motor Vehicle Administrators), which provide the inter-state and federal-state data-exchange contracts that DMV-issued attributes traverse. Each consuming engagement may require a per-state legal-basis citation in the entitlement; some states permit only narrow uses.

Three primary functions:

1. **Driver-license / state-ID disclosure observation.** Captures DL / state-ID disclosure events — license class, expiration date, REAL-ID compliance flag, restrictions, endorsements — via the documented AAMVA channels, emitting canonical claims with the per-state authority citation.
2. **REAL-ID compliance attestation.** Records whether the disclosed credential satisfies REAL ID requirements (gold-star or equivalent state marker), which is the canonical attribute for federal-facility access decisions post-2025 enforcement.
3. **Per-state entitlement enforcement.** For each disclosure, validates that the consuming engagement has the per-state legal-basis citation recorded in `canon/reciprocal-consumption-registry.yaml`; engagements without the citation are rejected before any AAMVA call.

**Implementation:** Reserved slot per ADR-049. Activation requires a per-adapter ADR modeled on ADR-035.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `drivers-license-verification`, `state-id-verification`, `real-id-compliance`

**Reads:** State-operated AAMVA channels for driver-license and state-ID disclosure events. Access gated by the per-state legal-basis citation and the consuming-engagement entitlement; both recorded in `canon/reciprocal-consumption-registry.yaml`.

**Emits:** `dmv-attribute-response.json` (per-disclosure canonical claim with credential class, expiration, REAL-ID flag, issuing state, restrictions), `dmv-disclosure-audit.json` (per-disclosure audit record with per-state legal-basis citation, entitlement reference, AAMVA channel id), plus `EvidenceObject` records that feed the IA-2 / IA-4 / AU-2 control evidence stream.

**Does NOT:** issue or modify driver licenses or state IDs (state DMVs are the authorities of record), bulk-pull DMV records (per-subject disclosures only), access driving-history or violation records outside what the disclosure surfaces, or operate across states without the per-state entitlement.

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `IA-2`, `IA-4`, `AU-2`, `AU-12`, `SC-8`, `SC-13`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **IA-2** Identification and Authentication | Supporting | DL / state-ID claims supplement identification when a government-issued credential is required. |
| **IA-4** Identifier Management | Primary | State-issued credential number is the stable identifier per disclosure (per-state namespace). |
| **AU-2** Event Logging | Primary | Every disclosure is logged with the canonical disclosure-audit envelope, per-state legal-basis citation, AAMVA channel id. |
| **AU-12** Audit Record Generation | Supporting | Audit records carry the canonical provenance envelope per ADR-006. |
| **SC-8** Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity | Primary | All AAMVA exchanges are TLS-protected. |
| **SC-13** Cryptographic Protection | Supporting | Validation uses FIPS-validated cryptographic libraries. |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `TBD` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `TBD` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `baseline` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

State DMVs are the per-state authorities of record for driver licenses and state IDs under the REAL ID Act of 2005. Inter-state and federal-state attribute exchange flows through state-operated AAMVA channels. Each consuming engagement may require a per-state legal-basis citation in the entitlement; some states permit only narrow uses. Activation requires a per-adapter ADR modeled on ADR-035.

Member of the federal attribute-service family.

ZTMM pillars: identity, data.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-003
- ADR-049 — Reserved-slot allocation framework

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
